segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: I haven't seen you for a bit, Marie.
segments.zh: 玛丽，我有一阵子没见到你了。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: No. I've been busy with my project.
segments.zh: 是啊，我一直在忙我的项目。

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: You're making a vegan alternative to eggs, aren't you?
segments.zh: 你在做一种鸡蛋的纯素替代品，对吧？就是那种不使用动物成分的东西？

words.def: 纯素的；严格素食主义的

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Something that doesn't use animal products?

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的，我用鹰嘴豆。刚开始寻找鸡蛋替代品时，我有两个主要目标，但实际上我发现鹰嘴豆有更多优点。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: I'm using chickpeas.

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: I had two main aims when I first started looking for an alternative to eggs, but actually I've found chickpeas have got more advantages.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Right.

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: But how about your project on reusing waste food – you were looking at bread, weren't you?
segments.zh: 那你那个关于再利用废弃食物的项目呢——你当时在研究面包，对吧？

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。工作很辛苦，但我很喜欢。基本过程相当直接——把不新鲜的面包打碎成糊状，然后重新塑形。但你当时用的是3D打印，对吧，把糊状物做成饼干？

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: It's been hard work, but I've enjoyed it.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: The basic process was quite straightforward – breaking the stale bread down to a paste then reforming it.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: But you were using 3-D printing, weren't you, to make the paste into biscuits?

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: Yeah, I'd used that before, but in this project, I had time to play around with different patterns for the biscuits and finding how I could add fruit and vegetables to make them a more appetising colour, and I was really pleased with what I managed to produce.
segments.zh: 对，我以前用过，但在这个项目中，我有时间尝试不同的饼干图案，并研究如何添加水果和蔬菜使它们颜色更诱人，我对自己的成果非常满意。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: It must've been a great feeling to make something appetising out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away otherwise.
segments.zh: 用那些本来会被扔掉的面包边角料做出美味的东西，那感觉一定很棒。

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: It was.
segments.zh: 确实。我希望镇上的一些餐馆会对这些饼干感兴趣。我打算给他们寄些样品。

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: And I'm hoping that some of the restaurants in town will be interested in the biscuits.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: I'm going to send them some samples.

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: I came across something on the internet yesterday that might interest you.
segments.zh: 我昨天在网上看到个东西，你可能会感兴趣。是一家公司，他们为食品标签开发了触敏传感器。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: It was a company that's developed touch-sensitive sensors for food labels.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Mmm?

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: It's a special sort of label on the food package.
segments.zh: 这是一种贴在食品包装上的特殊标签。标签光滑的时候，食物是新鲜的；当你摸到标签上有凸起时，就说明食物变质了。它最初是一个帮助视障人士判断食物是否可食用的项目。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: When the label's smooth, the food is fresh and then when you can feel bumps on the label, that means the food's gone bad.

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: It started off as a project to help visually impaired people know whether food was fit to eat or not.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Interesting.
segments.zh: 有意思。所以只适用于固体食物？

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: So just solid food?

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: No, things like milk and juice as well.
segments.zh: 不，像牛奶和果汁之类的也可以。不过实际上，我觉得它可能对医院和药房的药品储存很有用。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: But actually, I thought it might be really good for drug storage in hospitals and pharmacies.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。回到食物的话题，也许除了新鲜度，它还能用于其他方面。比如，一块肉有多少公斤之类的。

words.def: 一大块肉（通常用于烤制）

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: And coming back to food, maybe it'd be possible to use it for other things besides freshness.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Like how many kilograms a joint of meat is, for example.

items.prompt: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?

pairs.note: 题干中的most satisfying对应原文的a great feeling，都表示满足感。
题干中的make something appetising直接出现在原文中，是原词复现。

pairs.q: out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away
pairs.p: out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away
pairs.note: 题干中的out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away与原文完全一致，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接对应题干中的两个要点：'make something appetising'和'out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away'，同时'most satisfying'被替换为'a great feeling'。

items.prompt: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?

pairs.note: 'most satisfying' 对应 'a great feeling'，都表示满足感。

pairs.p: make something appetising out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away
pairs.note: 题干概括为 'bread reuse project'，原文具体描述用旧面包做美味食物。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'a great feeling' 对应 'most satisfying'，而 'make something appetising out of bits of old bread' 解释了面包再利用项目的内容。

items.prompt: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?

pairs.note: 题干中的‘developed in future’被替换为‘maybe it'd be possible’，表示可能性。
题干中的‘other things besides freshness’在原文中完全复现，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的‘touch-sensitive sensors for food labels’和‘other things besides freshness’在原文中直接复现，以及‘developed in future’被替换为‘maybe it'd be possible’，可以定位到原文中关于未来可能性的讨论。

items.prompt: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?

pairs.note: 题干'developed in future'对应原文'maybe it'd be possible'，表示未来可能性。
题干'ways'对应原文'for other things besides freshness'，指其他用途。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问未来开发触摸传感器的两种方式，定位句提到'可能用于其他东西，比如肉的重量'，因此答案应围绕'其他用途'和'重量'等具体例子。

items.prompt: Use of branded products related to celebrity chefs
Development of 'ghost kitchens' for takeaway food
Use of mushrooms for common health concerns
